text: **Anna:** This {article|記事/論文} is so interesting.
**Anna:** Pete, listen to this.
**Anna:** Somewhere in the world there is someone who looks just like you.
**Pete:** No one is this {handsome|ハンサム/格好いい}.
**Anna:** Oh Pete, there's something between your {teeth|歯}.
**Pete:** Is it gone?
**Anna:** No, no, it's still there.
**Anna:** Just the There you got it
**Anna:** But think of it, there could be two of us in the world.
**Anna:** Two anas?
**Anna:** Today we're {reviewing|復習する/見直す} ways to describe and compare people.
**Anna:** Keep watching for {descriptions|描写/説明} of people.
**Pete:** Actually, Anna, maybe your {silly|馬鹿げた/くだらない} article is right.
**Pete:** Yesterday at work, I met a woman who looks just like you.
**Anna:** Really?
**Anna:** Wait, are you making fun of me?
**Pete:** This time.
**Pete:** She's tall and has {curly|カール状の} hair, but even her face looks like yours.
**Pete:** I think she's a {career|キャリア/職業} consultant.
**Anna:** I bet she has a lot of great {advice|助言/提案}.
**Anna:** I can't wait to meet her.
**Pete:** Yes, you can.
**Pete:** I met her and she is very {difficult|難しい/厄介な}.
**Anna:** Pete, she's just new.
**Anna:** We have to give her a chance.
**Anna:** What's her name?
**Pete:** Eva Lena or something like that?
**Pete:** You can't miss her.
**Pete:** She always wears a hat.
**Penelope:** So Anna, have you met the new {consultant|コンサルタント} yet?
**Anna:** No, but I've already {heard|聞いた/知っている}
**Anna:** We {look alike|似ている|/lʊk əˈlaɪk/|We look alike in this photo.}, don't we?
**Penelope:** Yeah, but you don't {act alike|同じように行動しない}
**Penelope:** You're much
**Penelope:** {Nicer|より親切|/ˈnaɪsər/|She is nicer than her sister.} than she is.
**Anna:** Maybe she gets {nervous|緊張する|/ˈnɜːrvəs/|I feel nervous before a test.} at new jobs.
**Anna:** Or maybe she's {shy|恥ずかしがり屋の|/ʃaɪ/|She is shy around strangers.}
**Anna:** Let's not {judge|判断する/評価する}
**Anna:** She's not nervous or shy.
**Penelope:** She's {mean|意地悪な|/miːn/|Don't be mean to others.}
**Anna:** You know, we should {get to know|知り合いになる}
**Anna:** Let's invite her.
**Anna:** To lunch.
**Penelope:** Sorry, but I'm busy that day.
**Anna:** Oh, that's too bad.
**Anna:** Hey Wait, I haven't {picked|選んだ} a day yet.
**Anna:** So, did you find anything?
**Anna:** Here are a few things I found.
**Penelope:** Anna uses the word.
**Anna:** {look alike|似ている} to talk about the new consultant.
**Anna:** She says, we look alike, don't we?
**Anna:** Penelope uses then
**Anna:** And the {comparative|比較の} adjective {nicer|より親切} to compare Anna with the new consultant.
**Penelope:** She says, You're much nicer than she is.
**Pete:** Keep watching for more.
**Anna:** This is going to be so much fun.
**Anna:** Boy, I can't wait to {play|遊ぶ/プレイする} this game.
**Evilana:** How much longer are you going to be?
**Anna:** We'll just be a minute
**Anna:** You must be {Evolana|人名}
**Pete:** I'm Anna.
**Anna:** We are {look-alikes|そっくりな人|/lʊk-əlaɪks/|We are look-alikes, but not twins}.
**Evilana:** No, we're not.
**Evilana:** And my name is {pronounced|発音される|/prəˈnaʊnst/|How is your name pronounced?} Evolana.
**Anna:** Oh
**Anna:** This is going to be {fun|楽しい|/fʌn/|Let's have some fun today}.
**Evilana:** You had better {hurry up|急ぐ|/ˈhʌri ʌp/|Hurry up or we'll be late}.
**Evilana:** I need this room for a {lecture|講義|/ˈlɛktʃər/|The lecture starts at 2 PM}.
**Evilana:** It's called If You Want to Win, Others Must Lose.
**Evilana:** What is all this {stuff|もの|/stʌf/|What stuff do you have?} anyway?
**Anna:** It's a game.
**Anna:** We're going to play at lunch.
**Evilana:** It's a {stupid|馬鹿な|/ˈstjuːpɪd/|That's a stupid idea}.
**Anna:** Ha ha.
**Anna:** It's a fun game.
**Anna:** But yes, it is for children.
**Evilana:** Well you may be {childish|子供っぽい|/ˈtʃaɪldɪʃ/|Stop being so childish}.
**Evilana:** And you look stupid with that thing on your head.
**Anna:** Oh yeah?
**Anna:** Well you look serious with that
**Anna:** Look on your face.
**Anna:** Come on, Pete.
**Anna:** Let's go play somewhere else.
**Pete:** Actually, I'm going to stay for Evilana's lecture.
**Anna:** Fine
**Anna:** You can learn new ways to {beat|打ち負かす|/biːt/|Can you beat your opponent?} people.
**Evilana:** He will.
**Anna:** Okay, am I a food?
**Penelope:** Nope.
**Pete:** Hey, do you guys have room for one more?
**Penelope:** Sure Pete.
**Penelope:** Come on over
**Penelope:** What happened to your {eye|目|/aɪ/|My eye hurts today.}?
**Pete:** {Evil|悪い/意地悪な|/ˈiːvəl/|Evil people do bad things.} Anna accidentally hit me.
**Pete:** And she didn't even say {sorry|ごめんなさい|/ˈsɒri/|I am sorry for my mistake.}.
**Pete:** She's so {mean|意地悪な/冷酷な|/miːn/|Don't be mean to others.}.
**Penelope:** I told you so.
**Anna:** Here, Pete
**Anna:** Put on this {headband|カチューシャ/髪飾り|/ˈhedˌbænd/|She wears a colorful headband.}.
**Anna:** You'll feel better.
**Anna:** Penelope, you're right.
**Anna:** {Evilana|固有名詞|/ˈiːvɪlɑːnə/|Evilana sounds like a nickname.} is an {awful|ひどい/最悪の|/ˈɔːfəl/|That was an awful mistake.} person.
**Penelope:** And {violent|暴力的な|/ˈvaɪələnt/|Violent behavior is dangerous.}.
**Penelope:** And now we have to work with her.
**Anna:** Haven't you {heard|聞いた/知っている|/hɜːrd/|Have you heard the news?}?
**Pete:** She's gone.
**Anna:** She was given another {assignment|課題/仕事|/əˈsaɪnmənt/|My assignment is due tomorrow.}.
**Penelope:** What assignment?
**Anna:** I don't know.
**Anna:** I think it has something
**Anna:** to do with {outdoors|屋外/野外|/ˈaʊtdɔːrz/|We love playing outdoors.}.
**Anna:** Anyway, let's play.
**Anna:** Pete, it's your turn.
